name: How can I tell if my window installer knows coastal weather issues?

mainEntity.name: What questions should I ask to know whether a window installer understands Pacifica and Half Moon Bay weather exposure?
mainEntity.text: What questions should I ask to know whether a window installer understands Pacifica and Half Moon Bay weather exposure?

acceptedAnswer.text: A contractor who works in coastal areas should be able to explain more than just the window brand. Ask how they handle flashing, sill pan details, exterior sealants, corrosion-resistant hardware, and protection against wind-driven rain. They should also describe how they inspect the existing opening for rot or hidden moisture damage before installing the new unit. If the installer cannot explain how they prevent leaks in exposed locations, that is a warning sign. Coastal work often demands better detailing than a standard inland installation, especially in Pacifica and Half Moon Bay where wind and moisture can be severe. A good installer will also tell you which materials they prefer for salty, damp conditions and why. The goal is to hire someone who treats the window as part of the building envelope, not just as a product swap.
acceptedAnswer.description: Ask how they handle flashing, water intrusion, salt-air corrosion, wind-driven rain, and trim repair. A qualified coastal installer should explain product choices, installation methods, and how they prevent leaks in exposed locations.
